questions.question: Define Decision Tree Learning and explain its basic structure.
keyPoints: Tree-structured classification model
Internal nodes test attributes
Branches represent outcomes
Leaf nodes assign class labels
Mimics human decision-making

questions.question: What is Entropy? Explain with formula and state its range.
keyPoints: Measures randomness or impurity of dataset
Formula: -Σ p(x) log₂ p(x)
Ranges from 0 (pure) to 1 (impure for binary)
0 means all instances belong to single class

questions.question: Explain Information Gain in decision tree learning.
keyPoints: Measures reduction in entropy after splitting
Information Gain = Entropy(parent) - Weighted Entropy(children)
Attribute with highest information gain is selected for split
Helps in building pure subsets

questions.question: Differentiate between Pre-pruning and Post-pruning in decision trees.
keyPoints: Pre-pruning stops tree construction early (early stopping)
Post-pruning removes sub-trees after full tree built
Pre-pruning may cause underfitting
Post-pruning more common in practice

questions.question: What is overfitting in decision trees? How can it be prevented?
keyPoints: Overfitting occurs when tree learns noise in training data
Results in poor generalization to new data
Prevention methods: pruning, setting minimum instances per leaf, limiting tree depth
Tree becomes too complex with many branches

questions.question: Explain the ID3 algorithm for decision tree learning.
keyPoints: ID3 uses information gain for attribute selection
Greedy algorithm that builds tree top-down
Selects attribute with highest information gain at each node
Works well for categorical attributes
May overfit with many attributes

questions.question: What are the advantages and disadvantages of decision tree learning?
keyPoints: Advantages: Easy to interpret, handles both numerical and categorical data, requires little preprocessing, can handle missing values
Disadvantages: Prone to overfitting, greedy approach may not find optimal tree, small changes in data can change tree drastically

questions.question: Explain the process of building a decision tree using Information Gain. Illustrate with an example showing how entropy is calculated for attribute selection.
keyPoints: Start with entire dataset at root node
Calculate entropy of target variable
For each attribute, calculate weighted average entropy of resulting subsets
Calculate Information Gain = Parent Entropy - Weighted Child Entropy
Select attribute with highest Information Gain as splitting criterion
Repeat recursively for each child node until pure subsets or stopping criteria met
Example showing binary classification with entropy calculation

questions.question: Explain Gini Index as an attribute selection measure. How does it differ from Information Gain? Provide comparative analysis.
keyPoints: Gini Index measures impurity of dataset
Formula: 1 - Σ p(x)² for all classes
Ranges from 0 (pure) to 0.5 (max impurity for binary)
Used by CART algorithm
Difference: Gini tends to select attributes with many values, Information Gain prefers attributes with fewer values
Information Gain uses entropy, Gini uses squared probabilities
Both aim to create pure subsets but use different mathematical measures

questions.question: What is pruning in decision trees? Explain different pruning techniques with their advantages and disadvantages.
keyPoints: Pruning reduces tree complexity to prevent overfitting
Pre-pruning: stops early using criteria like minimum instances, maximum depth, information gain threshold
Post-pruning: builds full tree then removes sub-trees (e.g., reduced error pruning, cost complexity pruning)
Advantages of pruning: reduces overfitting, improves generalization, simpler trees
Disadvantages: may lose important information if over-pruned
